<!-- Generated by scripts/sync_chebfun_examples.py. -->
<!-- Source: https://www.chebfun.org/examples/ode-linear/AdvDiffJump.html -->

<div class="chebfun-import chebfun-example-import">
<div class='page-header'>
<h1>Advection-diffusion equation with a jump</h1>
<h2>Nick Trefethen, November 2010 in <a href='../'>ode-linear</a><span><a href='/examples/ode-linear/AdvDiffJump.m'>download</a><span class='sep-sm'>&middot;</span><a href='//github.com/chebfun/examples/blob/master/ode-linear/AdvDiffJump.m'>view on GitHub</a></span></h2>
</div>

<div id='content' class="col-sm-12 example" role="main">
<p>The solution to the advection-diffusion problem</p>
<p>$$ 0.2u'' + u' = -1, ~~    u(-10) = u(10) = 1 $$</p>
<p>has a boundary layer at the left:</p>
<pre class="mcode-input">LW = 'linewidth'; lw = 2; FS = 'fontsize'; fs = 8;
N = chebop(-10,10);
N.op = @(u) 0.2*diff(u,2) + diff(u);
N.bc = 'dirichlet';
u = N\-1;
plot(u,LW,lw), grid on
axis([-10.1 10 0 20])</pre>

<p><img src="../../images/ode-linear/AdvDiffJump_01.png" class="figure chebfun-figure" alt=""></p>
<p>Suppose the advection is only turned on on the right half of the domain?</p>
<pre class="mcode-input">figure
N.op = @(x,u) 0.2*diff(u,2) + (x&gt;=0).*diff(u);
N.bc = 'dirichlet';
v = N\-1;
plot(v,'r',LW,lw), grid on
axis([-10.1 10 0 75])</pre>

<p><img src="../../images/ode-linear/AdvDiffJump_02.png" class="figure chebfun-figure" alt=""></p>
<p>For fun we can plot both solutions on the same axis.</p>
<pre class="mcode-input">plot(u,'b',v,'--r',LW,lw), grid on
axis([-10.1 10 0 75])</pre>

<p><img src="../../images/ode-linear/AdvDiffJump_03.png" class="figure chebfun-figure" alt=""></p></div>
        </div>
    </div>
</div>
</div>
